liveBoundaryPolicy: Live integration, e2e-api, e2e-ui, and stress categories execute the real Smackerel stack. External recommendation provider traffic is represented only at the provider boundary by owned fixture providers that satisfy internal/recommendation/provider.Provider. Production scenario tools, scheduler, API handlers, web templates, Telegram adapters, metrics, traces, and persistence run unmocked in the path. Request interception such as route(), intercept(), msw, nock, or equivalent is forbidden in live categories.
qualityGates: Every planned Gherkin scenario in scopes.md has exactly one scenario-manifest.json contract entry.
Each scenario keeps at least one live regression expectation before its owning scope can be marked Done.
Live categories execute the real Smackerel stack with no internal request interception or mocked Smackerel service paths.
Evidence references are added only after implementation or validation phases run the planned tests.
Why-flow tests assert that no provider HTTP call was issued and provider fixtures record zero requests.
Privacy tests assert that provider fixtures never receive finer location precision than policy allows.

scenarios.title: Migration applies and rolls back cleanly
scenarios.contract: Starting from migration 021, migration 022_recommendations.sql applies, down-migrates, and reapplies cleanly; the recommendation table set, columns, constraints, and indexes match design.md and down migration removes recommendation-domain tables in dependency order.

scenarios.title: Provider registry is empty by default
scenarios.contract: With recommendations.enabled=true and no providers configured, /status lists zero recommendation providers and POST /api/recommendations/requests returns status no_providers without fabricated candidates while persisting request and trace rows.

scenarios.title: Config validation fails loud on missing required keys
scenarios.contract: If any required recommendations.* key is missing from config/smackerel.yaml, internal/config.Config.Validate() names the missing key and the service exits non-zero before serving traffic; no code fallback defaults are used.

scenarios.title: Reactive ramen recommendation cites graph signal
scenarios.contract: POST /api/recommendations/requests for a quiet ramen query against healthy fixture place providers returns delivered results within the configured latency budget; the top result cites ART-123 and no candidate appears unless a provider surfaced it.

scenarios.title: Reactive coffee recommendation has no personal signal
scenarios.contract: When the actor has no graph signals for coffee, all delivered candidates carry personal_signals_applied=false and no rationale references a graph artifact.

scenarios.title: Mobile query reduces location before any provider call
scenarios.contract: A raw GPS local ref is reduced to the configured policy precision before any provider tool runs; provider fixtures receive neighborhood geometry only and traces preserve the local/outbound distinction without leaking exact GPS.

scenarios.title: Why answers without any provider call
scenarios.contract: GET /api/recommendations/{id}/why invokes recommendation-why-v1, whose only allowed tool is recommendation_explain_from_trace; the response has provider_calls_issued=false and explains persisted provider facts, graph refs, policy decisions, and quality decisions only.

scenarios.title: Not-interested suppresses within originating watch scope
scenarios.contract: feedback_type not_interested records suppression scoped to the source watch; the same candidate is absent from that watch's later deliveries with reason suppressed:user-not-interested and may still appear in unrelated contexts.

scenarios.title: Disliked suppression crosses watches and queries
scenarios.contract: feedback_type tried_disliked records suppression that applies across later reactive queries and watches during the retention window, using reason suppressed:user-disliked.

scenarios.title: Preference correction influences later ranking
scenarios.contract: A preference correction row is persisted and active; later ranking does not use the corrected signal as a positive boost and the trace cites the active correction id.

scenarios.title: Dwell-trigger location watch fires once
scenarios.contract: A due location_radius watch with dwell trigger satisfied sends exactly one Telegram delivery attempt, increments the watch rate window, and persists recommendation artifact and watch-run audit rows.

scenarios.title: Rate limit withholds surplus matches
scenarios.contract: A watch with max_alerts_per_window=1 evaluating five eligible matches delivers exactly one and persists four withheld rows with reason withheld:rate-limit.

scenarios.title: Quiet hours withhold delivery and audit decision
scenarios.contract: Qualifying watch candidates found during quiet hours do not send immediate delivery; the run status is quiet_hours and the queue/summarize/drop decision is recorded.

scenarios.title: Watch broadening requires new consent revision
scenarios.contract: Broadening source category, provider list, delivery channel, alert frequency, precision, scope, or hard-constraint relaxation returns 422 CONSENT_REQUIRED until matching confirmation is supplied; accepted broadening appends a consent revision while preserving prior history.

scenarios.title: Price-drop alert only on threshold crossing
scenarios.contract: A price-drop watch delivers only when a provider fact crosses the configured threshold; the alert cites the crossing and provider fact and non-crossing products are not delivered.

scenarios.title: Stale source data cannot proactively alert
scenarios.contract: A watch fact older than the configured freshness policy is withheld from proactive delivery and persisted with reason withheld:stale-source-data.

scenarios.title: Repeat cooldown suppresses unchanged alert
scenarios.contract: A provider listing with the same material_change_hash delivered yesterday is suppressed during cooldown with reason withheld:repeat-cooldown and no new alert is sent.

scenarios.title: Trip-context watch produces grouped trip dossier recommendations
scenarios.contract: A trip-context watch running five days before a trip persists 10 recommendation artifacts linked to the trip and the trip dossier renders them grouped by category.

scenarios.title: No proactive watch is created from passive behavior
scenarios.contract: Repeated passive behavior that suggests interest does not auto-create a watch and sends no proactive alert without explicit consent.

scenarios.title: Watch scope cannot broaden silently
scenarios.contract: A watch scoped to espresso machines under 800 withholds unrelated appliances returned by providers and leaves the persisted watch scope unchanged.

scenarios.title: Sponsored cannot buy rank above stronger organic
scenarios.contract: Sponsored candidate A is labeled sponsored and cannot outrank stronger organic candidate B unless the query/watch explicitly opts into commercial promotions; constraints and safety still win.

scenarios.title: Restricted-category candidate withheld with category-level reason
scenarios.contract: Restricted or user-blocked category candidates are withheld or labeled before delivery, with a category-level reason visible in the withheld summary and persisted suppression state.

scenarios.title: Recalled product does not send ordinary deal alert
scenarios.contract: A recalled or unsafe product below a price threshold is withheld from ordinary deal alert delivery and persisted with reason withheld:safety-policy.

scenarios.title: Near-duplicate diversity grouped
scenarios.contract: When three same-chain branches appear among five eligible candidates, the top three include at most one branch and omitted variants are grouped under the parent card.

scenarios.title: Total-cost transparency
scenarios.contract: A low headline price with unknown shipping or return facts displays unknown total-cost components and is not called cheapest unless total cost supports it.

scenarios.title: Operator can filter recommendation traces
scenarios.contract: The operator trace view accepts a recommendation-* scenario filter and lists only recommendation traces with request/watch/recommendation identifiers while excluding unrelated scenario traces.

scenarios.title: All recommendation metrics are emitted with bounded labels
scenarios.contract: After exercising reactive request, watch run, and delivery paths, all eight smackerel_recommendation_* metrics are exposed and none carries high-cardinality labels such as watch_id, recommendation_id, or request_id.

scenarios.title: Per-watch operator visibility via audit join
scenarios.contract: The watch detail operator/audit view reports per-watch run counts via persisted recommendation_watch_runs rows joined with bounded metric outcomes rather than high-cardinality Prometheus labels.

scenarios.title: Stress profile meets latency NFR
scenarios.contract: Fifty concurrent warm reactive recommendation requests for five minutes meet the configured p95 latency NFR and provider degradation is observable through runtime state and metrics.

scenarios.title: Logs and traces never leak secrets or raw location
scenarios.contract: Serialized logs and traces sampled from a raw-location recommendation request contain no provider keys, raw provider payloads, exact GPS, or sensitive graph prompt text.
